subject: feat(ux): comprehensive UX, mobile, and accessibility improvements
body:
Addresses 30 UX issues across touch targets, mobile usability,
accessibility, and general polish for non-technical users.

Touch targets & buttons:
- Increase all button min-heights to 40-48px (WCAG 2.5.5)
- Fix notification bell, move up/down, delete, breadcrumb touch targets
- Add min-h-[44px] to form inputs and selects

Mobile UX:
- Module editor: reduce rows 16→8, add sticky bottom save bar
- Add unsaved changes warning (beforeunload + confirm on back)
- Fix inverted responsive text sizes (text-base sm:text-sm → text-sm)
- Remove autoFocus from modal forms (prevents keyboard pop on mobile)
- Fix notification panel width (use min() for viewport-safe sizing)
- Fix header safe-area-inset-top for notched phones
- Add PDF mobile hint ("best results on desktop")

Accessibility:
- Add focus trap to Modal (Tab/Shift+Tab cycling)
- Add skip-to-content link
- Add prefers-reduced-motion support (disable animations)
- Increase breadcrumb link tap targets with padding + hover states
- Contextual empty state icons (project/agreement/module/notFound)

Performance:
- Add React.lazy code splitting for all route pages
- Suspense fallback with loading spinner

Design polish:
- Relative timestamps in notifications ("3 min ago" vs absolute)
- Larger status badges (px-2.5 py-1 vs px-2 py-0.5)
- Allow title wrapping instead of truncation (mobile-readable)
- Desktop agreement grid (sm:grid-cols-2)
- Reduced empty state padding on mobile (py-8 vs py-12)
- Toast positioned higher with safe-area buffer
- PWA install prompt delayed 30s to let users engage first

Tags: ux, mobile, accessibility, pwa, performance
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: ux-polish
Semver: minor
